content.full_text:
April 10, 1981


Eric Hedlund, Supervisor
County of Humboldt
Eureka, CA 95501                A-81-04-019.

Dear Eric:

     The purpose of this letter is to confirm in writing our
telephone conversation of April 6, 1981. You had asked in
an earlier letter whether your interest in real property at
Blue Lagoon would require you to disqualify yourself from
participating in decisions of the Humboldt County Board of
Supervisors in regard to the Northeast Area Plan. Most of
the issues on the Northeast Area Plan have already been
decided by the Board. One of the issues which has not yet
been decided is the issue of a seismic ordinance which will
determine how close to the edge of a coastal bluff development
will be permitted. Since the Big Lagoon Park Corporation,
which owns the property at Big Lagoon, holds property along
a stretch of coastal bluff, you have a potential conflict of
interest as to this issue.

     As I explained on the phone, as a matter of policy we
refrain from giving advice to public officials with respect
to past actions. Under Government Code Section 83114, no
immunity can be granted for acts which occurred before advice
was requested. Since the purpose of written advice is to
give guidance as to future actions, I will not attempt to
advise you as to the issues which have already been decided.

     It is my understanding that you will request advice on
the seismic ordinance issue after the ordinance has been
drafted. We agreed that advice at this time would be premature
because we do not yet know the precise question which will
be put before the Board. Furthermore, since this is basically
a safety ordinance, the issue, as it finally comes before
the Board, may pose no conflict of interest for you.

     Please let me know if I can be of any further help.

                                    Very truly yours,


                                    Stella Connell Levy
                                    Counsel
SCL:kp                             Legal Division

January 27, 1981


Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA. 95804

Attention: Sarah T. Cameron
Deputy Chief for Conflicts
of Interest, Legal Division

Dear Miss Cameron:

This is a request for an opinion from the FPPC as to whether
I have a financial interest which might disqualify me from
participating as an office holder in decisions regarding the
Humboldt County Local Coastal Plan or the Humboldt County
General Plan.

My office is County Supervisor, District Five. As my statement
of interest shows, I reside at Big Lagoon, which is in the
coastal zone. The cabin is on land owned by the Big Lagoon
Park Corporation, Inc. By contract of purchase, my wife
and I are buying the cabin and a share of stock in the
corporation. The stock allows its holder to have a residence
on corporation land. Upon satisfaction of the terms of the
contract of sale, my wife and I may become stockholders in
the corporation, with the approval of its board of directors.
In the meanwhile, we are allowed to participate in shareholders'
meetings by assignment of proxy. If we should sell our
home, we must meet all terms of the contract of purchase,
and any profit would belong to my wife and myself, or our
assigns.

The corporation land and our cabin is located within the
region considered by the Northeast Area Plan which when
adopted will become a portion of the Humboldt County Local
Coastal Plan and General Plan.

Our equity in the contract of purchase agreement on our home
and the stock is in excess of $1,000 - and less than $10,000.

Continued . . .

FPPC
January 27, 1981
Page 2


It is unclear to me whether I have any financial interest in
the land owned by the Big Lagoon Park Corporation, Inc.
That land cannot be further divided, and there are very
thorough restrictions controlling any further improvement
of the structures on the land. No additional structures
can be added.

I have observed that over the past four years the average
sale price of a cabin and stock has moved from about $25,000
to about $35,000. This is behind the prevailing prices for
similar property in our county, but does indicate some
response to market conditions and inflation. I believe the
low price of this coastal property is attributable to the
horizontal segregation of interest in the land from interest
in the structure: Corporation bylaws do not even allow
building fences, on the premise that such a rule is necessary
to discourage residents from feeling like they own a separate
lot.

We also own a rental house in Eureka at 325 "O" Street,
within the city limits and the coastal planning area. There
is a first deed of trust outstanding on this real property,
again our equity is more than $1,000 - and less than $10,000.

If on the basis of this letter and my statement of interest,
you determine I have a financial interest sufficient to
disqualify me from some decisions in regard to our Local
Coastal Plan or the Humboldt County General Plan, I will
appreciate your indication of what land use decisions I may
participate in and what is the preferred course of action
when a land use plan comes before our Board of Supervisors.

If a copy of our contract of purchase agreement or the Big
Lagoon Park Corporation Articles and Bylaws would be material
to your decision as to my financial interest, please advise
me and I will provide you with these documents or any other
information you require.

Thank you for your consideration and advice.

Sincerely,

[signature]
ERIC W. HEDLUND
Supervisor, District 5

(707) 677-3229 - home
(707) 445-7695 - work

sections.question: You had asked in an earlier [letter] whether your interest in real property at Blue Lagoon would require you to disqualify yourself from participating in decisions of the Humboldt County Board of Supervisors in regard to the Northeast Area Plan.
sections.conclusion: Since the Big Lagoon Park Corporation holds property along a stretch of coastal bluff, you have a potential conflict of interest as to the seismic ordinance issue. However, we refrain from giving advice to public officials with respect to past actions. Since the purpose of written advice is to give guidance as to future actions, we will not attempt to advise you as to the issues which have already been decided. It is understood that advice at this time would be premature because we do not yet know the precise question which will be put before the board. Furthermore, since this is basically a safety ordinance, the issue, as it finally comes before the Board, may pose no conflict of interest for you.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a County Supervisor's financial interest in property at Blue Lagoon (owned by Big Lagoon Park Corporation) requires disqualification from participating in Board decisions regarding the Northeast Area Plan and related coastal/general planning matters.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC identifies a potential conflict of interest regarding the seismic ordinance issue due to the corporation's coastal bluff property. However, the FPPC declines to provide advice on past decisions and considers premature to advise on the seismic ordinance until it is drafted and the precise question is known, as it may ultimately pose no conflict.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains two components: an incoming request letter (January 27, 1981) and FPPC response letter (April 10, 1981). Response letter has poor OCR quality affecting some readability. No formal QUESTION/CONCLUSION sections but substantive advice provided. Incoming request shows clear financial interest disclosure details.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: You had asked in an earlier [letter] whether your interest in real property at Blue Lagoon would require you to disqualify yourself from participating in decisions of the Humboldt County Board of Supervisors in regard to the Northeast Area Plan.

CONCLUSION: Since the Big Lagoon Park Corporation holds property along a stretch of coastal bluff, you have a potential conflict of interest as to the seismic ordinance issue. However, we refrain from giving advice to public officials with respect to past actions. Since the purpose of written advice is to give guidance as to future actions, we will not attempt to advise you as to the issues which have already been decided. It is understood that advice at this time would be premature because we do not yet know the precise question which will be put before the board. Furthermore, since this is basically a safety ordinance, the issue, as it finally comes before the Board, may pose no conflict of interest for you.

embedding.summary: A Humboldt County Supervisor requested FPPC advice on whether his financial interest in property owned by Big Lagoon Park Corporation would disqualify him from participating in Board decisions on the Northeast Area Plan. The FPPC confirmed a telephone conversation declining to advise on issues already decided but identifying a potential conflict regarding an undrafted seismic ordinance issue.
